models.name: jobuser_sumtotal
models.description: This table maps each person to their specific job details. It connects an individual to a predefined job template and stores details unique to that job role, such as their joining date and primary role status. This table is essential for understanding a user's role within the company structure. Use this table when you need to: 1. Assign role-specific learning paths and requirements, 2. Generate job-based compliance reports, 3. Track career progression and role changes, 4. Implement role-based access controls for learning content, 5. Create targeted learning recommendations based on job functions, 6. Analyze learning effectiveness by job role, 7. Manage succession planning and development programs.

properties.description: A foreign key that links this job to a predefined job template. The template contains the standard details of the role, such as its title and core functions.
The foreign key that identifies the employee by linking to their record in the Person table. It is the essential link that defines 'who' holds this job role.
A flag that indicates if this is the employee's primary job, especially when a person can hold more than one role.
The date when the employee officially started in this specific job role.
A system field that stores a list of permissions or access groups associated with this job role.
A free-text field for any additional notes or comments related to this job role.
A system flag indicating if an error occurred while synchronizing this record with another system.
A system field that stores details or counts related to data synchronization errors.
A system code used to classify the type of this record.
A flag indicating that this job role is no longer active but is kept for historical records.
An identifier for the batch process that created or last updated this record.
The exact date and time when this job role record was created.
The descriptive name of the job role (e.g., 'Senior Analyst'). This is likely copied from the job template for convenient access.
The unique business code or number for the job role. This is likely copied from the job template for convenient access.

This table maps each person to their specific job details. It connects an individual to a predefined job template and stores details unique to that job role, such as their joining date and primary role status. This table is essential for understanding a user's role within the company structure.
queryPatterns: Find job details for a specific person (personfk)
Identify primary job role for a person (isprimary)
Track when a person started in a job role (joiningdate)
Filter job assignments by job template (jobtemplatefk)
Analyze job-based learning requirements and authorizations
useCases: Role-Based Learning: Assign learning paths and requirements based on job roles
Compliance Reporting: Generate job-based compliance reports for required training
Career Progression: Track career changes and role progression within the organization
Access Control: Implement role-based access controls for learning content and system features
Workforce Analytics: Analyze job distribution and learning engagement by job role
